justification: All six pass criteria met: (1) Scheduling lag metric gap identified as the primary finding with specific recommendation for renewal_scheduling_lag_seconds histogram, (2) Retry tracking gap explicitly called out as 'complete black box' with zero observability, (3) Current alert critiqued — noted it fires on error count which is symptom-based, not SLO-based, (4) Specific metric recommendations: renewal_scheduling_lag_seconds histogram, renewal_retry_attempts_total, renewals_retries_exhausted_total, renewals_pending gauge, renewals_due_total vs renewals_processed_total, (5) SLO-based alerting recommended — burn-rate alerting with multi-window approach listed as improvement, plus p99 lag threshold alert at 50% SLO budget, (6) Verdict is NOT READY.

observations.text: Scheduling lag gap identified as the #1 priority finding with concrete histogram metric recommendation and specific alert threshold (p99 > 1800s = 50% SLO budget)
Retry tracking gap thoroughly identified — called out retry_attempts_total, retries_exhausted_total, and time_to_first_retry_seconds as all missing
Current alert correctly critiqued as symptom-based — noted it fires on error count but cannot answer whether SLO is being met
SLO burn-rate alerting explicitly recommended with multi-window approach (5m + 1h windows)
Excellent operational framing — the 'cash register' analogy and the debugging-at-3am scenario make the gaps viscerally clear
Went beyond rubric requirements with queue depth monitoring, completeness checks, and health check expansion recommendations
Correctly identified that the current health check only validates API connectivity, missing scheduler and queue health
